svg.font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
svg.text: Option B — AEM Project Archetype: Maven multi-module + content package deploy
spa-mvn/ — what a production team ships. Generates promotable .zip artifact, integrates with Cloud Manager / CI.
1 · Source — Maven multi-module project (generated by aem-project-archetype:49 via maven-archetype-plugin 3.2.1)
2 · Build — mvn -PautoInstallPackage clean install (reactor builds modules in dependency order)
≈ few MB — same file promotes to all envs
3 · AEM Author JCR after package install (localhost:4502)
HTL render → clientlib serve → Vue mount
5 · Promotion — SAME spa-mvn.all-0.0.1.zip flows through environments
Same .zip every time. Runmode-scoped OSGi configs (in ui.config) supply per-env values
(API URLs, credentials, feature flags) without rebuilds.
Version stamped into Maven artifact = traceable from prod back to git SHA.
+ Reproducible build from git — same .zip every time, traceable to commit SHA
+ One artifact promotes through dev → CI → stage → prod (incl. Cloud Manager)
- Heavy initial setup (JDK + Maven + ~500 MB downloads on first build)
- Slower iteration loop (~25-60 s vs 5 s for the curl-only path)
Use Option A to learn JCR & experiment fast
Use Option B to ship real projects to real envs
